channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: England's right-back crisis continues - what are the options vs Norway?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: England’s defensive defiance in Mexico City was magnificent. But next comes the reality check: Norway in Miami with a patched-up back-line.

item.title: The robotaxi law that could ban Tesla
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: For more than a decade, one question has loomed over the race to build autonomous vehicles: Are cameras alone enough to safely replace human drivers, or do truly driverless cars need additional, overlapping sensors like lidar and radar to navigate the world reliably? Tesla has bet billions of dollar

item.title: It was the world’s hottest stock market. Now South Korea has entered bear territory.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The cumulative impact of several different narratives – competition, sustainability, excessive leverage, international outflows- has sapped the Korean market of its upward mobility since clocking up a record high in June.

item.title: Wave of Earnings Surprises Will Be Hard to Repeat, Says Goldman
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The wave of AI-fueled earnings surprises that powered stocks in the last earning season will be tough to repeat, making it unlikely the results alone will spark a major rally, according to Christian Mueller-Glissmann.

item.title: India Seeks Safe Hormuz Passage for Nine Ships as Truce Falters
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: India is considering options including holding talks with Iran in order to secure safe passage for at least nine laden tankers waiting in the Persian Gulf, as attacks in the waterway threaten an already fragile interim ceasefire.

item.title: Wildfires rage across southern France
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Firefighters in France are still fighting large wildfires, with a blaze in Pyrénées-Orientales in the south of the country not yet fully contained with 12,000 people evacuated. Fires are also raging In the Drôme and the on the island of Corsica.

item.title: Russia strikes Kyiv as Zelensky, Trump set to meet
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Russian strikes on Ukraine overnight killed at least two people while Kyiv targeted sites in Russia in drone attacks just hours ahead of a meeting between US President Donald Trump and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelensky at a NATO summit. France 24's Gulliver Cragg has the latest.

item.title: Trump says Iran agreement is over as airspace warnings signal growing danger
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Donald Trump, United States president has declared that Washington’s interim understanding with Iran is effectively over, dismissing any further negotiations read more Trump says Iran agreement is over as airspace warnings signal growing danger

item.title: Nasarawa police confirm abduction of three students of fed varsity
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "The prompt response of the police led to the successful rescue of one of the abducted students. Efforts have since been intensified to ensure the safe rescue of the remaining two victims still in captivity and the arrest of the perpetrators" it reads in part. The post Nasarawa police confirm abduct

item.title: Bayelsa father, son arrested for unlawful possession of firearms
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: By Shedrack Frank A 68-year-old man and his 36-year-old son have been arrested for alleged unlawful possession of four single-barrel firearms by operatives of Bayelsa Police Command. This is contained in a statement on Wednesday in Yenagoa, by the command’s Public Relations Officer, DSP Musa Mohamme

item.title: Crushing Nigeria’s illicit alcohol trade before it costs more lives
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Peter Egwuatu Nigeria’s campaign against illicit alcohol deserves to be seen for what it truly is, not simply a fight against counterfeit drinks, but a battle to protect consumers, safeguard legitimate businesses and defend the country’s economic future. The recent efforts by the Spir

item.title: US Stock Futures Slide After Trump Says Iran Ceasefire Is Over
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: US equity futures fell as investors turned risk averse after President Donald Trump said the tentative ceasefire with Iran has ended, fueling concerns of a renewal in fighting.

item.title: PFIPC scandal: Arrest of Adeyemi’s father cannot be justified under the law – Falana
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Femi Falana criticises the police’s handling of the PFIPC scandal, stating the arrest of Adeyemi’s father cannot be justified under Nigerian law.
Read More: https://punchng.com/pfipc-scandal-arrest-of-adeyemis-father-cannot-be-justified-under-the-law-falana/

item.title: Trump says ceasefire ‘over’ after US, Iran trade attacks
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Trump made the announcement at a NATO summit after Iran attacked bases hosting US forces in Kuwait and Bahrain.
